title: ATM/POS Transactions Failing
symptoms: postilion portal shows transaction processing errors
eft transactions remain in pending or failed state
checks: check postilion realtime monitor for node status (arrows should be black and nodes gray)
verify zimswitch interchange status and connectivity
inspect postilion events and logs for transaction-related errors
verify afcintellect node connectivity and error status
check sql server memory utilization; note if memory usage exceeds 90 percent
verify postilion license status and expiry dates
likely_causes: postilion realtime service degradation or failure
zimswitch interchange outage or interface disruption
sql server memory exhaustion affecting transaction processing
recommended_actions: follow the relevant sop-b procedures for controlled postilion service restarts.
validate postilion license expiry dates using sop-b-008.
confirm zimswitch connectivity and escalate if interface is down.
if sql server memory usage exceeds 90 percent, follow sop-b procedures for sql service restart.
escalate to idc support if afcintellect node reports persistent errors.
notes: this is a critical incident during business hours and requires immediate escalation
always verify both live and dr nodes: 192.168.0.91 (live), 192.168.254.91 (dr)
transaction queries in the postilion portal can be used to review the last 10 transaction statuses
avoid repeated restarts without identifying the underlying cause
